title: Cylindrolobus aporoides
intro: Cylindrolobus aporoides is a species of plant in the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as a type of flowering orchid tree. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may aid in wound healing and the management of infections. The bioactive constituents of Cylindrolobus aporoides include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Cylindrolobus aporoides is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures to treat various ailments, with the most common preparations being decoctions and extracts.
taxonomy: Cylindrolobus aporoides is a species of fungus in the family Cylindrolobaceae. It belongs to the phylum Ascomycota. This fungus is classified under the kingdom Fungi. The classification of Cylindrolobus aporoides is further refined under the class Leotiomycetes and the order Helotiales. It is a type of lichenicolous fungus, which grows on lichens.
